title: Call Stack
text: Call Stack View the ProcScript call stack to see the modules that are called or activated at runtime, or to identify where to put a try-catch block to handle exceptions. When script modules are called or activated at runtime, the call is added to the ProcScript call stack, along with their associated component names and line numbers. The most recent module appears at the top of the call stack. When a module is called recursively, a level of ProcScript nesting is added to the call stack. If the nesting level reaches 500, Uniface issues a fatal error message ( 9033 "Runtime error: Proc stack overflow" ). Fields The following fields are displayed in the debugger: Component —the name of the component in which the calling ProcScript module is located. Module —the name of the ProcScript module that has activated or called another module. Line —the line number of the activate or call command for the module that has called or activated another module. Code—the ProcScript code that performs the activation or call. Viewing the call stack To view the call stack, choose View > Call Stack from the debugger menu. To view a ProcScript module listed in the call stack, double-click it to open it in the Browser, or choose Show in Browser from the context menu. Note: It only possible to open components and Global ProcScripts, not application shells. Related Links ProcScript Call Stack When script modules are called or activated at runtime, the call is added to the ProcScript call stack, along with their associated component names and line numbers. The most recent module appears at the top of the call stack. Monitoring ProcScript Modules The Uniface Debugger allows you to monitor and control the execution of ProcScript. Debugger Browser Use the Debugger Browser to view the structure and ProcScript modules of components and Global ProcScript library. You can also toggle breakpoints in a ProcScript module, watch fields and variables, or change their values. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
